title: 10 best herbal teas for shingles
intro_desc: Herbal teas for shingles are a natural and comforting way to alleviate the symptoms of this painful and often debilitating condition. These teas, made from various herbs such as lemon balm, peppermint, and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help soothe the skin and reduce itching, pain, and discomfort. Drinking these teas can improve sleep quality, reduce stress, and boost mood, ultimately enhancing overall well-being and improving daily life for those affected by shingles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with shingles because of its antiviral properties that specifically target the herpes virus, which causes shingles. The tea's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the virus, reducing its ability to spread and cause further discomfort. Additionally, St John's wort teas may help alleviate symptoms such as pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles, providing a natural and soothing relief for those affected.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: St. John's Wort contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in St. John's Wort have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of shingle-related nerve pain and inflammation.

Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) properties: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to treat mild depression and anxiety, which is often exacerbated during a shingles outbreak; its anxiolytic properties may help alleviate emotional distress associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe the skin during shingles outbreaks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality st john's wort flowers and leaves from trusted sources.
Dry the freshly harvested st john's wort in a well-ventilated area or using machines for optimal potency.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried st john's wort per cup of boiling water and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container to remove the herb and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool before consuming, with recommended dosage being three cups daily for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with shingles because of its calming and soothing properties. The tea's active ingredients, such as citral and geraniol, work together to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with shingles. The antiviral properties of lemon balm also help to combat the underlying herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. Additionally, the tea's relaxing effects can promote a sense of calm and reduced stress, which are essential for managing the emotional toll of shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Lemon balm tea contains antiviral compounds such as citral and limonene, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including those responsible for causing shingles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon balm tea exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles lesions.

Antioxidant and soothing properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by inflammation, while its soothing effects can calm nerve endings and provide relief from postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), a common complication of shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are valued for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a great addition to teas for soothing shingle-related pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high levels of citral, a natural compound that can help reduce inflammation and ease nerve pain associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness and potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis herbs with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the tea immediately, or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain, itching, and discomfort associated with this condition. The tea's antiviral compounds also work to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, slowing down its replication and reducing the severity of outbreaks. Additionally, maidenhair tree teas' antioxidant properties help soothe irritated skin and promote healthy tissue repair, aiding in the recovery process and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Ginkgo biloba's antiviral compounds may help suppress the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in Maidenhair Tree tea may reduce inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with shingles, making it a potential adjunctive treatment for managing symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help mitigate oxidative stress and damage caused by the virus, which may accelerate the recovery process and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity of shingle symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every liter of water needed for steeping.
Boil the water in a tea kettle and add two teaspoons of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Enjoy the warm and soothing ginkgo biloba tea immediately after preparation while it is still at its optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with shingles because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate the excruciating nerve pain associated with shingles. The herb's sedative properties can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common companions to the condition. Additionally, valerian tea may help soothe inflammation and discomfort, allowing for a more restful sleep, which is often disrupted by shingles-related pain and itching. Overall, valerian teas can provide relief from the distressing symptoms of shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Valerian root has been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus, which can help reduce the severity and duration of shingle outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain associated with shingles rash, making it a valuable addition to a treatment plan for managing symptoms.

Sedative properties: Valerian tea has been traditionally used as a natural sleep aid, which can be beneficial in helping to manage the insomnia and restlessness that often accompanies shingle outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with Shingles.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which can help alleviate nerve pain and discomfort caused by the virus.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis root and measure out one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a pot until it reaches a rolling boil then turn off the heat.
Add the measured valeriana officinalis root to the hot water and let it steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth then discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with shingles because of its potent antiviral properties, which effectively combat the herpes virus that causes the condition. The tea's active compounds, such as capsicum, also possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles. Additionally, bell pepper tea's ability to stimulate circulation can help alleviate nerve damage and discomfort. By drinking bell pepper tea regularly, individuals can potentially reduce the duration and severity of shingles outbreaks, promoting a faster recovery and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties found in teas like echinacea, slippery elm, or licorice root: These properties can help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms by fighting off the virus and reducing inflammation.

Pain-relieving and antispasmodic properties found in teas like willow bark, chamomile, or passionflower: These properties may help alleviate the intense pain and discomfort associated with shingles outbreaks.

Immune-boosting and antioxidant properties found in teas like peppermint, ginger, or turmeric: These properties can help support the body's natural defenses against viral infections like shingles and reduce oxidative stress caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and itching associated with shingles.
Buds: The buds of the bell pepper plant have antibacterial properties that can help prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems, ensuring 2-3 ounces for every 8 ounces of water.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently under cool running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the washed plant material with 8 ounces of boiling filtered water in a large glass teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Store the cooled and strained tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, taking 1/2 cup servings every 4 hours as needed.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Kansas Coneflower contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against herpes viruses, including those responsible for shingles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles, making it a natural alternative to NSAIDs.

Immunomodulatory properties: Kansas Coneflower has been found to modulate the immune response by increasing cytokine production, which can help boost the body's natural defenses against viral infections like shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, roots are a natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent, making them effective in soothing shingle-related nerve pain.
Barks: Containing triterpenoids and other antioxidants, barks have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like herpes zoster, which is associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried echinacea angustifolia root bark for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a pot and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Steep the echinacea root bark in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, consuming two to three cups per day as needed.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with shingles because of its antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes the painful rash. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling and itching associated with shingles outbreaks. Additionally, kansas coneflower contains antioxidants that boost the immune system, helping to fight off the infection more effectively. By soothing and calming the skin, Kansas coneflower teas can provide relief from the discomfort and distress caused by shingles.

Yarrow teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which work together to reduce pain, itching, and discomfort associated with the condition. The tea's flavonoids and terpenes help to soothe the skin and nerves, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties may help to combat the virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. As a natural and gentle remedy, yarrow tea can provide relief without harsh side effects or complications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Yarrow tea's antiviral compounds, such as chromene and flavonoids, can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its replication and spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, including phenolic acids and sesquiterpenes, may help alleviate symptoms like pain, itching, and inflammation associated with shingles rash.

Immune-stimulating properties: Yarrow tea contains immunomodulatory compounds that can enhance the body's natural immune response, supporting its ability to fight off the viral infection and reduce the severity of shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in tea form to help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with shingles.
Flowers: The flowers are used in tea form to combat viral infections, including herpes zoster that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own plant.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbs in hot water for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce pain and discomfort associated with the condition. The tea's bioactive compounds, such as polysaccharides and flavonoids, have been shown to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of shingles. Antiviral properties: The antioxidants present in nettle tea have been found to inhibit the replication of viruses, including herpes simplex virus type 3 (HSV-3), which is responsible for causing shingles.

remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers and place them in a clean glass teapot.
Boil one cup of pure water and pour it over the urtica dioica mixture allowing it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large ceramic mug using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey to sweeten the tea if desired and stir well to combine.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly over a period of 10-15 minutes three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime teas helps with shingles because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the severity of the symptoms, such as pain, itching, and numbness. The antioxidants present in lime tea can also help to soothe the affected skin, reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, the acidity in lime tea may help to break down the nerve endings that cause shingles, providing relief from the burning sensation. Drinking herbal lime teas regularly may also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus that causes shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: The antiviral compounds present in herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and lemon balm can help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like ginger, turmeric, and lime zest contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with shingles, making it easier to manage symptoms.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing shingle symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks help reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote healing of skin lesions associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried tilia x europaea flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Boil 1 cup of pure water in a stainless steel pot for exactly 15 minutes to create a decoction.
Combine the boiling water with the tilia x europaea mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate glass container, discarding solids.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours to promote relief from shingles symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with shingles because it is believed to ease the painful symptoms associated with this viral infection. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and itching, providing relief from the uncomfortable rash. Additionally, fennel's antiviral compounds may inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. This natural remedy may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are essential for managing the emotional impact of shingles. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles.
Antiviral property: Fennel's antiviral properties can help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its replication and spread throughout the body.

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that may help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds from a trusted source and measure out one teaspoon per eight ounces of water.
Warm the water in a tea kettle or pot by boiling it for five minutes before turning off the heat.
Add one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds to the hot water and let it steep for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly and sweeten with honey if desired before consumption within thirty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with shingles because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity of symptoms such as itching, numbness, and burning sensations. The menthol in peppermint also helps to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea's antiviral properties may help to combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with shingles rash and lesions.

Antiviral properties: Peppermint's antiviral compounds, including menthol, may help inhibit the replication of the herpes virus (which causes shingles) and reduce viral load, thereby shortening the duration and severity of the outbreak.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Peppermint's natural pain-relievers, such as menthol and camphor, can help soothe and calm nerve pain, itching, and burning sensations associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots are used to soothe and calm irritated skin and nerve endings associated with shingles.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, peppermint barks help to reduce pain, inflammation, and itching caused by shingles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought organic peppermint leaves in a clean basket.
Rinse the peppermint leaves thoroughly under cool running water and pat them dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling filtered water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the essential oils and flavonoids to infuse into the water and release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving the tea hot with honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The tea's active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms by targeting the virus that causes it, herpes zoster. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can help alleviate the intense itching and pain associated with shingles, providing relief for those affected by this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Pot marigold tea's antiviral properties can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its ability to replicate and spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in pot marigold tea may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold tea's high antioxidant content can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by the shingles virus, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Seeds: Seeds contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate shingle symptoms such as itching and burning.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency for tea making.
Combine one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals with 8 ounces of boiling water in a large cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the calendula's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup, discarding the solids and retaining the flavorful infusion.
Enjoy the soothing calendula tea hot or warm throughout the day as needed, ideally in combination with other herbal remedies for optimal shingles relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles, making it a natural remedy for soothing the skin and relieving discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable source.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of the dried herb and place it in a teapot.
Pour boiling water over the eucalyptus globulus leaves and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming within 10-15 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and discomfort associated with the condition. The tea's camphor content also soothes nerve endings, alleviating itching and numbness commonly experienced during a shingles outbreak. remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: Wintergreen tea contains methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation associated with shingles.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wintergreen tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin and camphor, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with shingles lesions, promoting faster healing and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Rhyzomes: Contain wintergreen's signature compound, methyl salicylate, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and twigs or two teaspoons of fresh material.
Combine the harvested gaultheria procumbens with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids.
Consume the tea while warm, three times daily, and repeat as needed for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats teas helps with shingles because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with this condition. remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried oat straw, avena sativa, and add it to a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water and pour it over the dried oat straw in the cup immediately.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and reached a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids completely.
Drink the entire cup of tea while it is still warm, ideally three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers or purchase dried chamomile flowers in bulk store them in an airtight container.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or two teaspoons of fresh chamomile flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured chamomile flowers with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup steeping for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth discarding the solids.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container refrigerated for up to 3 days consuming at least three cups per day.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and flowers, ensure they are free of contaminants and pesticide residues.
Dry the sambucus nigra material in a warm oven or dehydrator for at least 30 minutes to preserve its properties.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried sambucus nigra with 1 cup of boiling water, steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discard the solids and reserve the liquid.
Drink the sambucus nigra tea immediately, repeat every 2-3 hours as needed or directed by a healthcare professional for optimal relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the pain, itching, and burning associated with this viral infection. The tea's antioxidants also help to soothe the skin and promote healing, reducing the duration and severity of shingle outbreaks. Additionally, dandelion's immune-boosting properties may help to support the body's natural defense against the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to traditional medical treatment for managing shingles symptoms.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with shingle outbreaks, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antiviral properties: The taraxasterol content in dandelion tea has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus type 1 (HSV-1), which is responsible for many cases of shingles.

Immunomodulation: Dandelion tea's immunomodulatory effects may help boost the body's natural defenses and stimulate a healthy immune response, potentially reducing the severity and duration of shingle outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Seeds: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral properties, helping to combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of dried dandelion root taraxacum officinale from a reputable source.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried dandelion root and place it in a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil one cup of pure filtered water in a pot for five minutes to create a hot beverage base.
Steep the dandelion root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to extract its beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately while it is still hot for optimal benefits.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shingles with herbal teas is by brewing a soothing blend of chamomile, calendula, and peppermint. Drink 2-3 cups daily to reduce itching, inflammation, and discomfort. Combine this with oat straw tea to ease the rash's itching and burning sensations. Echinacea tea can also be added to boost your immune system and aid in recovery.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shingles that are treated with herbal teas are herpes simplex, fever blisters, and cold sores. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and lemon balm have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce pain and discomfort associated with these conditions. Other herbs such as peppermint and ginger may also be used to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
